text.title: Reporting Dashboards Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every reporting-dashboards run must enforce. These are HARD rules — schedule pressure does not override them.

rule.statement: The fetch step uses a token scoped read-only on the PM tool. Write scopes are forbidden in the reporting pipeline.
rule.rationale: A reporter that can mutate the source produces blast radius far beyond its purpose; least-privilege is non-negotiable.

rule.statement: Report schema is a JSON file checked into the repo: status enum (On Track / At Risk / Off Track), threshold table, metric list, archival path, dashboard URL. Schema changes go through a PR.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc formats generate "what does this number mean?" questions; locked schema makes status auditable.

rule.statement: Health = function(completion_rate, scope_creep, blocked_count) — not single-metric. A 90% completion sprint with 10 mid-sprint scope adds is not healthy.
rule.rationale: Single-metric thresholds create false-positive health reports and erode trust.

rule.statement: Validation gate fires before delivery: committed>0, fetch_errors==0, sprint_start_date<=today. If any fails → error alert + exit non-zero. Never deliver a degraded report.
rule.rationale: A 0% completion report shipped because the query failed destroys reporting trust faster than no report.

rule.statement: Rendered report + raw JSON fetched are written to a durable store (S3 / Notion / repo path / GitHub issue comment) BEFORE delivery. Delivery includes the archive URL.
rule.rationale: Without archive, the report is ephemeral; recipients cannot audit, trends cannot be reconstructed.

rule.statement: Generation and delivery are separate processes / scripts / steps. Generate → archive → deliver. Never collapse them into one call.
rule.rationale: Coupling makes retry-after-Slack-fail impossible without regenerating; coupling also hides the validation gate.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
